item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I immediately understood what this was in reference to due to my misspent youth reading every single Percy Jackson book in existence.

Nice article. Not much coming from a nobody, but I was entertained.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Images removed due to non compliance

Lyre Source

Guitar Source

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this idea is very innovative! An object that can change reality can go in many directions and has a lot of potential, and the greed in taking it for oneself really hooked me in at the beginning.
I do feel like it could have been more, though. I think that, if reality had been changed numerous times rather than just the once, it could have been better. The brief appearance of some god who takes control of the instrument also feels a little…off, rather much like a dues ex machina. He doesn't really feel like he fits in with the story at all.
I think it would have been neat is something larger had happened during these reality events, something huge that was, at the end, resolved yet no one remembered what it was. Or maybe if it had explored a man's rise to abuse his sudden power would've been more interesting.
The presentation was amazing though, and really fit in with the break from one reality to the next. The video logs also helped show the audience the characters emotions better than the SCP report would have been on its own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The image will be released to you under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0) license.

Image by the British Museum

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I honestly enjoyed this article. I did find the multiple security measures to be a bit overblown (I can understand some memetic kill hazards for something that could warp the fabric of reality, but the multiple layers of it seemed a bit overdone), and the purpose of them was completely undone by a janitor accessing the lyre (and from the sounds of it, using it to institute a global "federation" whereby all citizens are of equal class).

One thing that does puzzle me is that this is a SAFE object even though the containment procedures state that it is to be put in a "KETER-class reality suppression locker". I mean, from the sounds of it this thing is capable of making anything happen, and from the second audio excerpt it threatened to kill everyone in existence. Should this be considered a KETER object?

However, I did enjoy the article! I thought it had a nice build-up from a random guy using the lyre to change the world into going further into detail as to who he was, what he created, and the problems he now faces. The last line about not needing an anomalous instrument to work magic was something I enjoyed, and serves to give some advice that can be used IRL. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey if I'm not around, please put this in the 4112 slot. If it's not available, go ahead and put it anywhere but my tale won't make too much sense continuity wise. If someone wanted to edit that that would be fine too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Better! Loads much faster. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've reduced the filesizes, and I'll now take care of that excess.

Edit: now it's relatively massive, I'll look at formatting to be smaller

Double edit: Should be fine now, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No worries!

Also, another note, maybe trim off that extra white space around the second picture. (I can do that too if needed.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll see what I can do. Thanks for letting me know that's an issue!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Author, your files total over 2MB. Any chance you can resize those so they don't take so long to load for mobile? It'll also help with viewership for readers on limited data plans.

If you would like me to resize the pictures for you, I can do that.

item.title: Re: Hey there!
item.content:encoded: I chose to use Cygnus instead of Cycnus because of the song of the same name, played by the same person, and also Cygnus here on the wiki. Additionally, the real link to Apollo is the tortoiseshell lyre, not his child, that was intended to be confirmation.

item.title: Re: Hey there!
item.content:encoded:
Not if you looked under "Cygnus". Cycnus, however.

Though, he isn't really a well known figure. The more relevant figure would be Orpheus, who were said to be inheriting the lyre of Apollo and became a swan as punishment. Wikipedia mentioned him as one of the possible swan depicted in the constellation "Cygnus"

item.title: Re: Hey there!
item.content:encoded:
A bit of digging before dismissing an unknown wouldn't be too out of line, would it?

Have you actually googled "Cygnus"? Nothing about Apollo comes up.

item.title: Hey there!
item.content:encoded:
I noticed you had some issues with my SCP. If I may, I'd like to address your concerns 1 by 1.

My personal style of writing is very trope heavy, and that's not limited to SCP tropes. In fact, I'm rather fascinated by them. On the other hand, I'm unsure how that directly creates a negative reading experience, so I'm going to file that under personal preference.

The scenario presented is a planned heist. While working the job of janitor, Alex saw this as a chance to "Save Humanity", again, a very cliched trope that I enjoyed exploring. He had one chance to clean the room it was in and he took it. The massively overblown containment procedures exist because this is one of the most dangerous single objects that I know of on the wiki. That's why my partner decided to implement a more rigorous protocol.

I do note that that line was incredibly vague, and I hope my update has solved some of your confusion.

The live updates were a way that I could implement a live commentary while reality was being shifted, as I think it would be even more of a suspension of disbelief to have a surviving log from moments before a reality wipe. Please also note that an inexperienced reality wielder would likely not be omnipresent enough to sense members of his staff plotting against him, especially when they're deliberately keeping it secret.

Additionally, many users besides yourself have caught on that this artifact links back to the Lyre of Apollo, and as such, the man of Mediterranean descent would conclusively be Apollo, who also has a son named Cygnus, mentioned in the text. A bit of digging before dismissing an unknown wouldn't be too out of line, would it?

Thank you for reading my article, and I hope this helped!

-Diana

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I found this to have far too many issues to be enjoyable, unfortunately.

For starters, this opens in a very trope-heavy way. You've got massively overblown containment procedures, then you list out the anomalous properties in a numbered list starting with the least relevant (which happens to be invincibility.) And then, with very few changes, you have almost the same text again.

If said user completes this while intending to do anything,

This line is pretty vague. Honestly, the entire description of the second effect is a little non-specific in all of its wording. The meaning does come across when you've read the entire thing, but it's very imprecise.

I find the idea of having live updates that may or may not even be relevant to the thing in a database entry to be a very odd conceit especially since reality is apparently being altered after the fact. And, ultimately I have no idea who this mystery man at the end is supposed to be so it just comes across as a deus ex machina. And, if this is a reality where this guy has altered everything as he sees fit, why would the documentation for the source of his power have a bunch of notes from this Research Team 9 expressing doubt and concern at everything without him doing something about it?

But, honestly, the biggest problem with all of this is that, even with the massively overblown containment procedures, somehow the janitor managed to get a hold of the thing? The concept itself isn't terrible and I think in different circumstances your whole fading text bit could work quite well, but ultimately this is a -1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yey

item.title: Re: All of the Rush references I saw
item.content:encoded: Also Smapti who wrote SCP-2112 and a few other Rush inspired things. Also, you might look into the edit I just made, new reference to catch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Made a few small edits. If anyone knows how to get that centering functional on the first live dictation go ahead, I did these edits on mobile.

Edit: MrBazzle ur a legend

item.title: Re: All of the Rush references I saw
item.content:encoded: Have you ever hung out with GreenWolf? Given that http://www.scp-wiki.net/unusual-investigations has tales named Permanent Waves, A Farewell To Kings and Moving Pictures, I think you'd get along. ;-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my, so many votes that it’s at 0…

…or not

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh. This one fell a little flat for me, I'm afraid.

The idea of an instrument, that when played allows a user to rewrite reality is an interesting one, but as a reader, I don't feel you really explore the concept enough, just a before and after glimpse that leaves me feeling like there is a particularly large hole in your narrative, as well as missing pieces as to the true power and potential of this artifact. You don't build up to a grand ending so much as you go straight from the piolet episode to the series finale.

Additionally, as a contest entry, the tie into the History theme seems tangential at best, since you don't really dive deep enough into the implications of wholesale rewriting history outside of a few cursory name and format changes.

-1. Solid idea, but the execution could use some work.

item.title: Re: All of the Rush references I saw
item.content:encoded: You missed some white text, buddy. Other than that, bravo. Also I've never heard Hemispheres, I think. That's really wacky. I'll have to look into it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article is very interesting and I like the direction it went. It held my interest for the entirety of my reading. +1.

item.title: All of the Rush references I saw
item.content:encoded:
+ All of the Rush references I saw
- You asked

The lead guitarist of Rush is Alex Lifeson.
Alex Lifeson played a white Gibson ES-335 on all Rush songs mentioned below, and still plays one sometimes.
The Rush song Hemispheres stars Apollo and Dionysus, and "Unidentified 1" is pretty clearly Apollo.
The Rush song 2112 involves the (Solar) Federation and its (Temple of) Syrinx being threatened by a guitar player, and ends with the words "We have assumed control".

Did I miss any?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: honestly I'd like to celebrate but I don't want to circlejerk it to high hell

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that Clef upvoted your entry is proof that God is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lets just go with yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do the sounds required to activate the second anomalous effect have to come from the instrument, or can it be any sound? Like, if I were to just hold it while singing or belching or playing a piano with my feet? While I'm sure the intent is that you have to actually play the instrument itself, I feel the description makes it more vague than it has to be. I read "Make a sound of any kind," and this is the direction my mind immediately goes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neat. I disagree that you need to explain more, everything is quite clear. I did spot some errors:

Personnel should ensure they are to ensure

with intent to make a sound, and intends to perform an act of any kind

That one's redundant as well. Also, there's a "Foundation" in the video log, when I believe this new place was called the "Federation".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's one of the things we had to work on the most. Originally we were planning on using embedded images but we noticed that it didn't perform very well on mobile devices, so we went with the text. We had to fiddle with it a bit there too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The visual presentation on this article is beautiful. I especially liked the fade and the feeling it gives off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! Explaining quite what happened to the world is a difficult and to be honest dangerous thing to do. For difficult, it's rather hard for me to slip in references to the outside world in casual conversation about a specific object, but I think we did our best. Also, being too specific in the politically charged atmosphere that is the wiki about what a "utopia" is is the dangerous bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was pretty cool. Although it does have some pretty nasty contenders, I belive it has potencial. You could add more to explain how the janitor changed the world. I likw the way you charactirized the Administrator. He isn't a socipath, but just a dude that gets shit done and understands more about the world than try to use it to make it his dream land. You got my upvote. :)

item.title: Top Text
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to We Have Assumed Control by ArmLengthRadius and GibberingEloquence.

Firstly, thanks are in order. I'd like to personally thank my running mate Gib, who has undoubtably chosen to undersell his participation in the text. I'd also like to thank both Zyn and Dr. Everwood for the helpful words of advice. And to you, who's reading our article.

EDIT: Westrin had some fine ideas about the fine tuning of our formatting, mad credit.

EDIT 2: We now have a tale! Maybe come check it out.

Sometimes perfection requires a caressing hand and not a calculated scalpel.

From Gib:

"Big kudos to ArmLengthRadius for writing most of the article and being a supportive person. Hope you all like it, and don't forget to check out Rush's songs, which were a big inspiration!"
